title: rosa pimpinellifolia subsp. myriacantha
intro: Rosa pimpinellifolia subsp. myriacantha is a subspecies of the wild rose plant, native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its fragrant flowers and thorny stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve respiratory issues. The health benefits of Rosa pimpinellifolia subsp. myriacantha include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its flowers and leaves can also be used in skincare products due to their antiseptic and astringent properties.
